data_vintage.note: DA-RT P(DA>RT) Smartbidder CSV file absent — Date column only, 6th consecutive day. Evening block P(DA>RT) sourced from market-analyst briefing citing Smartbidder DA-RT Forecast output (HE20 62.2%, HE21 64.9%, HE22 61.9%). Midday block P reconstructed from first principles.

meta.leakage_check: COMPLIANT — all sources pre 2026-05-26 10:00 CT DAM cutoff
meta.spread_sign_convention: spread = DA - RT; positive => DA expensive => Short DA / Long RT signal; negative => RT expensive => Long DA / Short RT signal
meta.product_type: DART Virtual financial position (INC/DEC) — fully separated from GKS BESS physical dispatch and AS bidding. No dependency on bess-optimizer decisions.
meta.confidence_discount_reason: Smartbidder P(DA>RT) CSV file absent (6th consecutive day). Evening block P partially anchored via market-analyst briefing Smartbidder reference (partial offset from 0.80 prior cycles to 0.85). Midday block P fully reconstructed without calibrated model.

model_inputs.comparable_cycle: 2026-05-24 (Sunday, GR_WEST 600 MW — same order of magnitude)
model_inputs.tuesday_dam_adjustment: none — normal weekday liquidity, no holiday discount

positions.rationale: GR_WEST 652 MW (near-zero); solar 11,514 MW rapidly rising; ERCOT wind 3,652 MW; net load 39,102 MW. WEST_TO_NORTH export constraint MEDIUM probability. HB_NORTH is import-receiving side — positive lambda when constraint binds drives RT above DA. Tuesday normal DAM — no holiday discount. P(DA<RT) ~62% reconstructed.
positions.risk_note: GR_WEST 652 MW slightly above HE11 trough; constraint may not fully bind at this hour; max adverse scenario ~$80 if spread flips +$3.20 adverse direction

positions.rationale: GR_WEST 628 MW — daily trough, equivalent to 2026-05-24 Sunday signal (~600 MW). Solar 14,470 MW; ERCOT total wind 3,589 MW; net load 37,829 MW entering trough. WEST_TO_NORTH binding MEDIUM (~22-30%) — when binding, HB_NORTH receives positive lambda; RT > DA. 2026-05-24 comparable cycle: LONG DA HE10-12 at HB_NORTH was likely win. Tuesday = normal DAM, no structural overprice discount needed. P(DA<RT) 65% reconstructed. E[spread] -$5.40 includes +20% bias correction.
positions.risk_note: If WEST_TO_NORTH does not bind (P ~70-78% non-binding), spread may be near zero or adverse. Stage 0 heuristic — PTDF not available. Max loss ~$135 at 50 MW if spread adverse -$5.40

positions.rationale: GR_WEST 632 MW; solar 16,893 MW; ERCOT wind 3,511 MW; net load 36,898 MW (approaching trough). Core window for WEST_TO_NORTH export congestion. HB_NORTH import-receiving side benefits from positive constraint shadow price. P(DA<RT) 64% reconstructed. E[spread] -$4.80 with +20% bias correction.
positions.risk_note: Same constraint risk as HE11. DA $21 at suppressed level — Long DA entry price is already low, limiting downside if spread slightly adverse.

positions.rationale: GR_WEST 640 MW; solar 18,597 MW near peak; ERCOT total wind 3,473 MW — lowest of day. Net load 36,665 MW (second lowest). Solar-wind double trough intensifies West export surplus. P(DA<RT) 61% — slightly above MED floor. Sized MED (25 MW) as load ramp starting by HE14 could relieve constraint early.
positions.risk_note: Signal attenuating — load ramp begins HE14+. If constraint eases faster than forecast, HE13 may be marginal. 25 MW MED limits exposure.

positions.rationale: Solar 12,050 MW still present but declining sharply. Net load 44,873 MW entering ramp. DA price estimated ~$31.50 (Smartbidder file unavailable; first-principles from ramp onset DA pattern). HOUSTON/SOUTH IMPORT constraint MEDIUM-HIGH (28-38%) transition entry. HE19 established as valid signal hour per 2026-05-22 self-review (skip was correct that cycle at thin spread; this cycle DA-RT wider). E[spread] +$3.60 with +20% bias correction. P 60% at HIGH threshold — borderline but Houston import binding window opening.
positions.risk_note: Solar 12 GW still suppressing RT partially. Ramp not fully established — RT spike asymmetric risk if load surprises upward. Transition hour: earliest entry in evening block.

positions.rationale: Smartbidder P(DA>RT) = 62.2% — highest-quality probability input available (market-analyst briefing citation from Smartbidder DA-RT Forecast). DA $54.75; market-analyst E[spread] +$10.71. Net load 49,836 MW; resource cap-out 16,924 MW (daily low) — ORDC scarcity possible but Tuesday demand is contained. HOUSTON/SOUTH IMPORT constraint MEDIUM-HIGH peak window. 2026-05-24 analog: HB_HOUSTON SHORT DA HE20 at $45.58 was likely win (+$5.58 spread). This cycle DA at $54.75 with wider expected spread. +20% bias correction embedded in E[spread].
positions.risk_note: ORDC tail risk: cap-out 16,924 MW + net load 49,836 MW = narrow reserve margin. Unplanned outage could push 5-min RT above $54.75 and beyond. Highest dollar value position in book.

positions.rationale: Smartbidder P(DA>RT) = 64.9% but ALSO P(DA<RT) = 51.9% (non-complementary; both signals from Smartbidder — indicates extreme bimodal distribution at DA $73.28). DA $73.28 is extreme — real risk that RT exceeds this (net load peak 53,670 MW, cap-out recovering to 17,244 MW). Bid price capped at $65.00 (not $73.28) to avoid settling into the RT spike scenario. Effective P adjusted to 57% after accounting for spike risk distribution. Sized MED (50 MW, below HIGH would be 50 MW regardless — treated as MED due to risk-adjusted P). E[spread] $18.00 from DA $73 → RT $55 midpoint assumption with +20% bias correction.
positions.risk_note: CRITICAL: If RT exceeds $65.00 (our bid price cap), this position is a loss. If RT exceeds $73.28 (market-analyst DA forecast), spread is severely adverse. P(DA<RT) 51.9% is material — this is not a low-probability risk. Monitor HE20 RT settlement before HE21 as leading indicator.

positions.rationale: Smartbidder P(DA>RT) = 61.9% — above 60% HIGH threshold. GR_WEST wind 3,311 MW recovering — RT suppressed post-peak. Net load 52,176 MW declining from HE21 peak. Houston import constraint easing but basis still positive. Tuesday: prior self-review (2026-05-24 learnings) identified HE22 upgrade from MED (25 MW) to HIGH (50 MW) as justified — P 62% is cleaner at this hour than the borderline 59% Memorial Day cycle. E[spread] $7.80 with +20% bias correction.
positions.risk_note: HE21 RT spike risk can carry forward to HE22 — if HE21 RT is elevated, HE22 RT may also be elevated. Wind building is the primary risk mitigant.

skipped_hours.HE01-09: Overnight wind 6,680-9,239 MW (GR_WEST 1,194-3,154 MW); DA $30-34 thin; spread flat HE1-8 avg +$0.15 (market-analyst). No directional conviction. GR_WEST still elevated HE09 vs trough at HE11. WEST_TO_NORTH signal not yet activated at HE09 (GR_WEST 725 MW at HE09 but solar only 7,782 MW still ramping — combined signal weaker than HE11-12). No settlement history for overnight Long DA positions per prior cycle learning.
skipped_hours.HE14: Net load trough 36,175 MW — looks good but GR_WEST 686 MW turning up from HE11 trough; load ramp starting HE14+ absorbs West surplus rapidly; congestion-analyst notes binding signal peaks HE11-13 not HE14. P signal weakening — skip.
skipped_hours.HE15-18: HE15-17: GR_WEST recovering (783-1,066 MW); load rising (64,486-66,066 MW); West surplus rapidly absorbed; WEST_TO_NORTH binding probability declines to LOW; DA-RT spread HE15-17 flat ($0.20-$0.51 avg). HE18: ramp onset RT spike asymmetric risk (solar 16,105 MW still present; net load 41,597 MW; transition to evening regime — preserved skip rule from all prior cycles).
skipped_hours.HE23-24: DA declining; wind recovering 8,112-8,371 MW; GR_WEST 3,956-4,349 MW; spread direction ambiguous; RT likely following wind down. No signal above 55%.

risk_factors.he21_rt_spike_primary: DA $73.28 is extreme. P(DA<RT) 51.9% from Smartbidder — if RT exceeds DA, Short DA HE21 loses. Bid capped at $65 but if RT is $75+, position is adversely exposed. Net load peak 53,670 MW + cap-out recovery to 17,244 MW is key uncertainty.
risk_factors.west_to_north_binding_miss: GR_WEST 628 MW is near-zero but PTDF unavailable. If constraint does not bind at HB_NORTH (70-78% probability), Long DA positions HE10-13 face adverse spread. Max book exposure: 150 MW × avg adverse spread ~$3 = -$450.
risk_factors.smartbidder_probability_absent: 6th consecutive day without Smartbidder DA-RT P(DA>RT) CSV. Evening block P partially anchored via market-analyst briefing Smartbidder reference. Midday fully reconstructed. Confidence discount 0.85 applied.
risk_factors.enverus_yes_energy_solar_gap: Enverus solar HE15 22,907 MW vs Yes Energy 20,225 MW. Higher solar -> lower net load -> WEST_TO_NORTH binding reduced (paradoxically weakens Long DA thesis). Lower solar realized (Yes Energy) -> ramp steeper -> stronger Long DA.
risk_factors.ordc_tail_risk_he20: HE20 cap-out 16,924 MW + net load 49,836 MW. Narrow reserve margin. Unplanned outage could fire ORDC -> 5-min RT spike. Short DA HE20-21 most exposed.
risk_factors.tuesday_dam_efficiency: No holiday adjustment needed. Tuesday is fully normal weekday DAM — positions sized at standard HIGH/MED tiers without holiday discount.

backtest_snapshot.seven_day_rolling_hit_rate: NOT CALCULABLE — DART virtual P&L not isolatable from Tenaska DA_Energy_Amt without separate virtual book ledger
backtest_snapshot.last_confirmed_directional_outcome: 2026-05-22: 5/5 known-direction hours correct (LONG DA HE12-14, SHORT DA HE20-21 all likely WIN)
backtest_snapshot.e_spread_bias_pattern: Realized spreads 15-31% above forecast in 2026-05-22 settlement. +20% correction applied all cycles since.
backtest_snapshot.high_tier_sizing: 50 MW HIGH tier established 2026-05-24 cycle (upgraded from 25 MW per 2026-05-22 self-review). Validation pending 2026-05-24 and 2026-05-25 settlement.
